hypothesis: Strict day-2 follow-through add-ons may still be under-sized because the accepted 35% add-on position cap blocks confirmed winners; lifting only the add-on cap to the existing 40% single-position cap could release lifecycle alpha without changing entry, exit, ranking, LLM/news replay, or global position count.
why_not_llm_soft_ranking: LLM soft-ranking remains high-upside but production-aligned replay samples are still too thin; this deterministic lifecycle allocation test is fully replayable and production-shareable through existing add-on constants.

market_regime_summary.mid_weak: rotation-heavy bull after accepted allocation stack
market_regime_summary.old_thin: mixed-to-weak older tape with weakest win rate

gate4_basis: Rejected for materiality. All three windows improved directionally, but aggregate EV delta +0.0745 stayed below the +0.10 experiment materiality bar and PnL improved only +$1,766.97 / about +1.6%, below the 5% PnL gate.

llm_metrics.blocker_relation: LLM soft-ranking was bypassed because available replay coverage remains insufficient for ranking attribution.

rejection_reason: The cap lift unlocks one extra executed add-on per window and is consistently positive, but the economic effect is too small to justify increasing concentration risk.
do_not_repeat_without_new_evidence: Global add-on cap lifts above the accepted 35% cap without a new concentration or event discriminator
Using directional-but-small add-on cap gains as production evidence
next_retry_requires: Forward evidence that cap-blocked add-ons are materially larger winners
A state/event discriminator that identifies which confirmed winners deserve extra concentration
Tail-risk evidence showing the higher cap does not worsen drawdown under weaker tapes
